controls.label: Does your business have a Customer Due Diligence — Individuals process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Customer Due Diligence — Individuals process in place. You must establish a documented Customer Due Diligence — Individuals process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include appropriate risk-based systems and controls designed to enable you to be reasonably satisfied that individual customers are who they claim to be?
controls.detail-label: Describe the risk-based systems and controls you use to establish reasonable satisfaction of customer identity
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure does not include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to establish reasonable satisfaction that individual customers are who they claim to be. Implement risk-based controls that enable you to assess and verify customer identity in accordance with the ML/TF risk the customer presents, as required under rule 4.2.2.

controls.label: Does your procedure collect the minimum KYC information for individual customers: full name, date of birth, and residential address?
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure does not collect the minimum KYC information required for individual customers. Update your process to collect full name, date of birth, and residential address for every individual customer before commencing a designated service, as required under rules 4.2.3(1), 4.2.3(2), and 4.2.3(3).

controls.label: Does your procedure collect the minimum KYC information for sole traders: full name, date of birth, business name (if any), address of principal place of business or residential address, and ABN (if any)?
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure does not collect the minimum KYC information required for sole trader customers. Update your process to collect full name, date of birth, business name (if any), address of principal place of business or residential address, and any ABN issued to the customer, as required under rules 4.2.4(1) through 4.2.4(5).

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to determine whether any additional KYC information (beyond the minimum) should be collected about individual customers?
controls.detail-label: Describe the risk-based approach you use to determine when additional KYC information should be collected
controls.remediation: Your Customer Identification Procedure does not include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to determine when additional KYC information should be collected beyond the regulatory minimum. Implement a risk-based decision framework that assesses whether extra information is needed based on the ML/TF risk profile of each individual customer, as required under rule 4.2.5.

controls.label: Does your procedure verify, at a minimum, the customer's full name and either their date of birth or residential address?
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity process does not verify the minimum KYC information required for individual customers. Update your process to verify the customer's full name and either their date of birth or residential address before commencing a designated service, as required under rules 4.2.6(1) and 4.2.6(2).

controls.label: Is your verification of customer information based on reliable and independent documentation, reliable and independent electronic data, or a combination of both?
controls.detail-label: Describe the sources of documentation and/or electronic data you use for verification
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity process does not base verification on reliable and independent sources. Ensure that all customer information verification relies on reliable and independent documentation, reliable and independent electronic data, or a combination of both, as required under rules 4.2.7(1), 4.2.7(2), and 4.2.7(3).

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to determine whether any additional KYC information (beyond the minimum) should be verified?
controls.detail-label: Describe the risk-based approach you use to determine when additional KYC information should be verified
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity process does not include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to determine when additional KYC information should be verified beyond the regulatory minimum. Implement a risk-based decision framework that assesses whether extra verification is needed based on the ML/TF risk profile of each individual customer, as required under rule 4.2.8.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program include appropriate risk-based systems and controls to respond to any discrepancy that arises during verification so you can determine whether you are reasonably satisfied the customer is who they claim to be?
controls.detail-label: Describe your process for responding to verification discrepancies
controls.remediation: Your Verification of Identity process does not include appropriate systems and controls to respond to discrepancies discovered during verification. Implement risk-based procedures to investigate and resolve verification discrepancies so you can determine whether you remain reasonably satisfied the customer is who they claim to be, as required under rule 4.2.9.

controls.label: Do you wish to use the documentary safe harbour procedure for medium or lower ML/TF risk customers?
controls.remediation: This is an optional safe harbour procedure. You may choose to implement it or use alternative verification methods that meet the requirements of rules 4.2.3 to 4.2.8.

controls.label: Do you wish to use the electronic safe harbour procedure for medium or lower ML/TF risk customers?
controls.remediation: This is an optional safe harbour procedure. You may choose to implement it or use alternative verification methods that meet the requirements of rules 4.2.3 to 4.2.8.

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: General Customer Due Diligence Obligation
groups.description: Your AML/CTF program must include appropriate risk-based systems and controls designed to enable you to be reasonably satisfied that individual customers are who they claim to be.

groups.title: Collection of KYC Information
groups.description: Minimum information requirements that must be collected for individuals and sole traders before commencing a designated service.

groups.title: Additional KYC Assessment
groups.description: Risk-based determination of whether additional KYC information beyond the minimum should be collected.

groups.title: Verification of Information
groups.description: Minimum verification requirements and methods for confirming the identity information collected from customers.

groups.title: Additional Verification Assessment
groups.description: Risk-based determination of whether additional KYC information beyond the minimum should be verified.

groups.title: Discrepancy Handling
groups.description: Systems and controls to respond when verification reveals inconsistencies or issues with customer information.

groups.title: Documentary Safe Harbour Procedure
groups.description: Optional prescriptive procedure for document-based verification when ML/TF risk is assessed as medium or lower. This provides a safe harbour method to comply with collection and verification obligations.

groups.title: Electronic Safe Harbour Procedure
groups.description: Optional prescriptive procedure for electronic verification when ML/TF risk is assessed as medium or lower. This provides a safe harbour method to comply with collection and verification obligations.
